task_completion.description: Verify the workbook is created with at least three sheets using professional clinical nutrition terminology.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application is open with the file named 'nutrition_analysis.xlsx'. At least three sheet tabs are visible at the bottom with professional names (e.g., 'Daily Intakes', 'ADA Comparison', 'Weekly Summary').

task_completion.description: Verify per-item intake calculations and daily totals for calories and key nutrients.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet rows/columns showing individual food items and calculated daily totals for calories, protein, carbs, fat, fiber, and sodium.

task_completion.description: Verify meal-level carbohydrate analysis flags entries exceeding 60g.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific column, text indicator, or conditional formatting (e.g., red text/highlight) explicitly flagging meal carbohydrate values that are greater than 60g.

task_completion.description: Verify comparisons against ADA dietary targets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section or table placing the patient's calculated daily or weekly intakes side-by-side with the ADA guidelines/targets for comparison.

task_completion.description: Verify a weekly summary showing average, minimum, and maximum daily intakes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A summary table or section explicitly displaying 'Average', 'Minimum', and 'Maximum' values for the 7-day nutrient intakes.

task_completion.description: Verify a section identifying nutritional problem areas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text notes, a summary box, or a dedicated section explicitly calling out specific nutritional issues, such as 'high-sodium days' or 'low-fiber intake'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a spreadsheet GUI to create the workbook.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a graphical spreadsheet application (like ONLYOFFICE or LibreOffice Calc) being used to edit the file, rather than a terminal window running a python script to bypass GUI interaction.

integrity.description: Verify the spreadsheet contains genuine data from the provided files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet should contain multiple rows of actual food diary entries and nutrient data, not just placeholder text or a single mocked-up row.

integrity.description: Verify the output is a genuine spreadsheet and not pasted text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data should be properly separated into spreadsheet cells and columns with headers, rather than a block of plain text or markdown pasted into a single cell.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient profile is a 55-year-old male, BMI 31, sedentary. The food diary covers exactly 7 days. The expected output file must be saved at /home/ga/Documents/Spreadsheets/nutrition_analysis.xlsx.
